zh.lifeSummary: 當Condesa De Cifuentes Aldonça Martins Da Silva於大約1184年出生在葡萄牙桑塔雷姆桑塔雷姆時，她的父親Martim Gomes Da Silva Cabrera28歲，母親Urraca Ruiz de Cabrera54歲。她與?結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1266年在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯布爾戈斯享年83歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安卡拉其德洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Condesa De Cifuentes Aldonça Martins Da Silva于大约1184年出生在葡萄牙桑塔雷姆桑塔雷姆时，她的父亲Martim Gomes Da Silva Cabrera28岁，母亲Urraca Ruiz de Cabrera54岁。她与?结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1266年在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯布爾戈斯享年83岁，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安卡拉其德洛。

nameMeanings.name: Martins
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Fernando, Francisco, Mario, Luis, Armando, Alvaro, Ana, Marcelo, Sergio.  Portuguese Joaquim, Joao, Paulo, Agostinho, Henrique, Afonso, Batista, Ademir, Amadeu, Anabela, Caetano, Heitor.
Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name Martim, from Latin Martinus (see Martin ).
Dutch and German: patronymic from the personal name   Martin  . In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the more common Dutch and North German variant   Martens  .

variants.name: Martin
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Martinz
meanings: Portuguese: variant of   Martins  .
Germanized form of Slovenian Martinc or of its variant Martinec .

nameMeanings.name: Da
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 達, said to be derived from the personal name Shu Da (叔達), a talent during the reign of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437  BC ); or adopted as a Han surname by other minority ethnic groups.
Mandarin form of the surname 笪, a surname originating in ancient Jianzhou (located in present-day Fujian province) and Jurong (located in Jiangsu province) or a surname from the Hui ethnic group.

nameMeanings.name: Silva
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Juan, Luis, Jesus, Mario, Fernando, Francisco, Pedro, Roberto, Miguel.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo, Joaquim, Agostinho, Duarte, Henrique, Serafim, Vasco, Ilidio, Adao, Afonso, Altair.

Portuguese, Galician, and Italian: habitational name from any of the many places called Silva in Portugal, Galicia (Spain), and Italy, or a topographic name from (Old) Portuguese and (Old) Galician silva ‘wood’, later ‘bramble’. Compare Da Silva and Silba .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Iberian surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Da Silva
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Fernando, Francisco, Luiz, Mario, Jorge, Julio, Marcelo, Roberto.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Paulo, Duarte, Agostinho, Henrique, Marcio, Nelio, Albano, Altair, Amadeu, Catarina.
Portuguese and Galician: variant of Silva , with the addition of da, a shortened form of the preposition de ‘from’ and the feminine definite article a ‘the’.

variants.name: Silba
meanings: Hispanic and Italian (Apulia): altered form of Portuguese, Galician, and Italian   Silva  .

variants.name: Silveira
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Luis, Fernando, Carlos, Pedro, Francisco, Mario, Ana, Joaquin, Jorge, Adolfo.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo, Altair, Duarte, Henrique, Joaquim, Serafim.

Portuguese and Galician: topographic name from silveira ‘woodland’, a collective derivative of silva (see Silva ); or a habitational name from any of the places called Silveira in Lugo and Pontevedra provinces, Galicia (Spain). Compare Da Silveira , Selvera , and Silvera .

variants.name: Sylva
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Fernande, Mathieu.
Altered form of Portuguese, Galician, and Italian   Silva  . Outside the US this surname is most common in Senegal and the Gambia, where it is of Portuguese origin.

variants.name: Silvas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Manuel, Raul, Jose, Lupe, Roberto, Alfredo, Armando, Domingo, Emilio.
Portuguese and Galician: topographic name from silvas, plural of silva ‘thicket, bramble’. See Silva .
Americanized form of Hungarian Szilvás: occupational name for a grower or seller of soft fruits, derived from szilva ‘plum’.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Condesa De Cifuentes Aldonça Martins Da Silva was born about 1184, in Santarém, Santarém, Portugal, her father, Martim Gomes Da Silva Cabrera, was 28 and her mother, Urraca Ruiz de Cabrera, was 54. She married ?. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1266, in Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain, at the age of 83, and was buried in Carracedelo, León, Castilla y León, Spain.
